content: Hey, I'm so exhausted. I've been taking care of my aging parents in Portland, OR, and working full-time as a Blockchain Developer at a DeFi protocol.
I'm so sorry to hear that. It sounds like you have a lot on your plate. How are your parents doing?

They're doing okay, considering. My mom has Alzheimer's, and my dad has Parkinson's. It's tough to see them like this, but I'm trying to stay positive. I've been trying to get them to move into a retirement community, but they're resistant to the idea.
That can be really challenging. Have you considered seeking out any support groups or resources to help you cope with caregiving?
Yeah, I've been looking into that. I've also been trying to make time for my own hobbies, like marathon running and leatherworking. It's hard to find the time, but it helps me clear my head.
That's great that you're prioritizing self-care. Marathon running is a big commitment - are you training for a specific race?
I'm actually training for the Portland Marathon in October. I've got about 6 months to go, so I'm trying to stick to a strict training schedule.
That's exciting! You must be really disciplined to balance your training with work and caregiving. Do you have a support system in place to help you with your parents while you're running?
Yeah, I've got a few friends who are willing to help out, and my colleague, Alex, has been really understanding about my situation. I'm lucky to have such a great team at work.
That's great to hear. It's always important to have a supportive network. Have you thought about what you'll do after the marathon? Will you take a break or start training for another race?
I'm not sure yet. I've been thinking about doing a triathlon next year, but I need to get my parents settled into a new routine first. Maybe by January, I'll have a better idea of what's possible.
